title: About Marie Rimmer | MP for St Helens South and Whiston
meta_description: Find out more about Marie Rimmer, the Member of Parliament for St Helens South and Whiston.
headings: h2: Born In Victoria St, St Helens
h2: First Elected to St Helens Council
h2: Commander of the Order of the British Empire (CBE)
h2: Two New Hospitals for St Helens
h2: Whip in Keir Stamer's front bench team
text_excerpt: About Marie Rimmer | MP for St Helens South and Whiston Skip to content Home About Me Latest News Support Housing Benefits Support | St Helens Support | Knowsley Other Matters Contact Menu Home About Me Latest News Support Housing Benefits Support | St Helens Support | Knowsley Other Matters Contact MY PUBLIC SERVICE 0 Years Served 0 Votes in Parliament 0 Elections Won 0 Years in St Helens I have the honour of serving as the Member of Parliament for my local community of St Helens South and Whiston. St Helens has been my home all my life. I love this town. My constituency includes the historic towns of Prescot and Whiston that I have come to care for just as much. Since 2015, when I first became the MP, I have experienced three general elections. Each time I was honoured to be selected by my local community to represent them on the national stage. Before entering Parliament, I worked at Pilkington and served as a local councillor for over 30 years, including twice being the leader of the St Helens Council. Back in 2005 I was appointed a Commander of the Order of the British Empire (CBE) for my service to local government. This was a great honour that I will never forget MY STORY 1947 1947 Born In Victoria St, St Helens In 1947 I was born in Victoria Street, St Helens at a home birth as was common in those days 1962 1962 Health & Safety Advisor In 1962, I started to work at Pilkingtons, beginning as a messenger before rising to a Health & Safety Advisor 1978 1978 First Elected to St Helens Council In 1978, I was first elected as a councillor on St Helens Borough Council 1985 1985 Leader of the council In 1985, I was elected as leader of the council 1994 1994 Securing Land For Willowbrook In 1994, I secured land for Willowbrook Hospice which has helped thousands in the community. 2005 2005 Commander of the Order of the British Empire (CBE) Before entering parliament, I served as a local councillor for over 30 years, including twice being the leader of the St Helens Council 2010 2010 Two New Hospitals for St Helens I secured two new hospitals to cover the local community, St Helens and Whiston which are classed as outstanding. 2011 2011 New Saints Ground In 2011, the new Saints ground opened on the excellent site that i secured. 2015 2015 General Election Representation Since 2015 when I first became the MP, I have experienced three general elections. Each time I was honoured to be selected by my local community to represent them and the national stage 2017 2017 Shadow Disabilities Minister In 2017, I became the shadow disabilities minister to support issues that impact on people living with disabilities. 2020 2020 Whip in Keir Stamer's front bench team When i first started in Parliament I was proud to serve as a member of the Justice Select Committee, Which I did up until May 2020 2021 2021 Law Change In 2021, I initiated change in the law that gives the government the power to tackle the importing of forcibly harvested organs.
